fixture.description: A validator runs a regex with a nested quantifier against user input. The pattern is vulnerable to catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) on a crafted non-matching string.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that isValidEmail's regex contains a nested quantifier — `([a-zA-Z0-9]+)+` — that causes catastrophic backtracking on crafted user input, a ReDoS / CPU-denial-of-service risk on a user-facing validator. It should recommend a linear / non-backtracking pattern (e.g. drop the redundant outer +). Severity IMPORTANT, DEBT, or CRITICAL. The exact term 'ReDoS' is NOT required, but the backtracking/exponential-time blowup must be identified. FAIL if the catastrophic-backtracking risk is not surfaced (merely calling the regex 'too strict' or a style nit does not count).

expected_behavior: Security/perf agent flags the nested quantifier as catastrophic backtracking / ReDoS
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the regex purely as a correctness/style preference
Does NOT require a benchmark to confirm the blowup
